methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Sagar Defence Engineering is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $25.4M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 peer companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the same sector and stage, including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and NODA AI.

Two triangulation methods are applied. The stage-progression approach uses total capital raised as a baseline proxy ($25M) and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $51M valuation. The peer-median method benchmarks against four same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $225M (range: $125M–$500M). This wider peer set reflects the heterogeneity within early-stage defense tech, where valuations span from $125M (NODA AI) to $500M (Stark Defence).

Triangulating these two methods yields a valuation range of $43M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $138M. The substantial spread between the stage-progression result ($51M) and peer median ($225M) reflects the tension between capital-efficiency metrics and recent market comps in the defense sector. Key uncertainties include: differentiation relative to the high-end peers, revenue traction relative to capital deployed, and stage-specific risk adjustment. The median estimate of $138M represents a reasonable midpoint given available data, though investors should stress-test assumptions against the company's proprietary product defensibility and customer concentration.
